text.title: Structured Output Rules and Failure Modes
text.summary: Validation retry strategy, production rules, and failure modes for structured output in agent pipelines.

rule.statement: Use `instructor` with `max_retries=3` for automatic retry. On each retry, inject the ValidationError as additional context — never retry with the same prompt at temperature=0.
rule.rationale: If validation fails once at temperature=0, it will fail again with the same input. The error message tells the model exactly what was wrong, enabling a corrective attempt.

rule.statement: Log every validation error with the raw model output. After 3 consecutive failures, escalate — the schema may be wrong, not the model.
rule.rationale: Persistent validation failures usually indicate a schema that is ambiguous, too complex, or misaligned with the task. Without raw output logs, you cannot diagnose whether the issue is in the prompt, schema, or model.

rule.statement: In multi-agent pipelines, always re-validate on receipt even if the sending agent validated on emit. Serialization can corrupt data between agent boundaries.
rule.rationale: JSON serialization/deserialization across agent boundaries can introduce type coercions (int to float), None handling differences, or encoding issues. Validate both sides of every boundary.

rule.statement: Never put schema JSON into user messages — put it in tool definitions or system prompt only. Version schemas in code (`class OutputV2(BaseModel)`) to track changes across deployments.
rule.rationale: Schema in user messages increases input token count and can be overridden by user-injected content. Tool definitions are processed separately from the conversation, making schema injection attacks impossible.

rule.statement: For extraction tasks, add a `raw_quote: str` field — forces the model to ground its answer in source text and dramatically reduces hallucinated extractions.
rule.rationale: Without a source quote, the model may invent plausible-sounding field values that are not present in the source. Requiring a verbatim quote creates a self-checking mechanism.

antipattern.description: Using JSON mode instead of structured output for production extraction pipelines.
antipattern.reason: JSON mode returns valid JSON syntax but may omit required fields, add unexpected fields, or use wrong types. These are silent failures that corrupt downstream state.

antipattern.description: Streaming responses with structured output: trying to parse the response incrementally.
antipattern.reason: Structured output validation requires the complete response. Streaming cannot be validated until the stream completes. Buffer the full response before parsing.

antipattern.description: Schema complexity compounds with context length: testing schema on short toy examples then deploying on production-length contexts.
antipattern.reason: A schema that works on 100-token inputs may produce validation errors on 8K-token inputs where the model's attention is spread across a much larger context. Always test at production context length.
